prompt:
A 100 g spool of 0.8 mm solder wire sits on an electronics bench, its shiny lead-free alloy neatly wound around a plastic reel. The free end of the solder extends slightly from the spool, resting near a small PCB and soldering iron stand, suggesting it is ready for prototyping work. The reel’s hub carries a simple icon-based label with no readable text, indicating lead-free composition without specifying exact alloy details.

The scene is a near-future solarpunk electronics workspace with soft, even lighting, an anti-static mat beneath the components, and a few organized bins of parts in the background. The 512×512 image is photorealistic with gentle stylization, no logos or legible writing, and a composition that centers the spool, loose solder end, and adjacent PCB while the rest of the bench and tools remain softly blurred.
